  [user_task_description] # Requirements Document for 'WeatherForecast' Web Application  ## 1. Objective Develop a comprehensive web application named 'WeatherForecast' using Python, with data managed through local text files. The application enables users to view current weather conditions, check weekly forecasts, search for locations, receive weather alerts, and monitor air quality. No authentication required - all features are directly accessible. Note that the website should start from the Dashboard page.  ## 2. Language The required development language for the 'WeatherForecast' application is Python.  ## 3. Page Design  The 'WeatherForecast' web application will consist of the following eight pages:  ### 1. Dashboard Page - **Page Title**: Weather Dashboard - **Overview**: The main hub displaying current weather, quick location access, and navigation to all functionalities. - **Elements**:   - **ID: dashboard-page** - Type: Div - Container for the dashboard page.   - **ID: current-weather-summary** - Type: Div - Display of current weather conditions for default location.   - **ID: search-location-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to location search page.   - **ID: view-forecast-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to weekly forecast page.   - **ID: view-alerts-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to weather alerts page.  ### 2. Current Weather Page - **Page Title**: Current Weather - **Overview**: A page displaying detailed current weather conditions for selected location. - **Elements**:   - **ID: current-weather-page** - Type: Div - Container for the current weather page.   - **ID: location-name** - Type: H1 - Display location name.   - **ID: temperature-display** - Type: Div - Display current temperature.   - **ID: weather-condition** - Type: Div - Display weather condition (sunny, rainy, cloudy, etc.).   - **ID: humidity-info** - Type: Div - Display humidity percentage.   - **ID: wind-speed-info** - Type: Div - Display wind speed.  ### 3. Weekly Forecast Page - **Page Title**: Weekly Forecast - **Overview**: A page displaying weather forecast for the next 7 days. - **Elements**:   - **ID: forecast-page** - Type: Div - Container for the forecast page.   - **ID: forecast-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying daily forecasts with date, high temp, low temp, and condition.   - **ID: location-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter forecast by location.   - **ID: forecast-list** - Type: Div - Grid displaying forecast cards for each day.   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ### 4. Location Search Page - **Page Title**: Search Locations - **Overview**: A page for users to search and select different locations. - **Elements**:   - **ID: search-page** - Type: Div - Container for the search page.   - **ID: location-search-input** - Type: Input - Field to search locations by city name or coordinates.   - **ID: search-results** - Type: Div - List of search results displaying matching locations.   - **ID: select-location-button-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to select a location (each result has this).   - **ID: saved-locations-list** - Type: Div - Display of previously saved locations.  ### 5. Weather Alerts Page - **Page Title**: Weather Alerts - **Overview**: A page displaying active weather alerts and warnings for selected locations. - **Elements**:   - **ID: alerts-page** - Type: Div - Container for the alerts page.   - **ID: alerts-list** - Type: Div - List of all active weather alerts with severity, description, and location.   - **ID: severity-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter alerts by severity (All, Critical, High, Medium, Low).   - **ID: location-filter-alerts** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter alerts by location.   - **ID: acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}** - Type: Button - Button to acknowledge an alert (each alert has this).  ### 6. Air Quality Page - **Page Title**: Air Quality Index - **Overview**: A page displaying air quality information and pollution levels for locations. - **Elements**:   - **ID: air-quality-page** - Type: Div - Container for the air quality page.   - **ID: aqi-display** - Type: Div - Display air quality index value (0-500).   - **ID: aqi-description** - Type: Div - Display air quality description (Good, Moderate, Unhealthy, etc.).   - **ID: pollution-details** - Type: Table - Table showing PM2.5, PM10, NO2, and other pollutants.   - **ID: location-aqi-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter by location.   - **ID: health-recommendation** - Type: Div - Display health recommendations based on air quality.  ### 7. Saved Locations Page - **Page Title**: Saved Locations - **Overview**: A page displaying all saved locations with quick weather access. - **Elements**:   - **ID: saved-locations-page** - Type: Div - Container for the saved locations page.   - **ID: locations-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying saved locations with current temp and weather condition.   - **ID: view-location-weather-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view weather for a location (each location has this).   - **ID: remove-location-button-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to remove saved location (each location has this).   - **ID: add-new-location-button** - Type: Button - Button to add new location.  ### 8. Settings Page - **Page Title**: Settings - **Overview**: A page for users to configure temperature units, notification preferences, and default location. - **Elements**:   - **ID: settings-page** - Type: Div - Container for the settings page.   - **ID: temperature-unit-select** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select temperature unit (Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin).   - **ID: default-location-select** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to set default location.   - **ID: alert-notifications-toggle** - Type: Checkbox - Toggle to enable/disable alert notifications.   - **ID: save-settings-button** - Type: Button - Button to save settings changes.   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ## 4. Data Storage  The 'WeatherForecast' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:  ### 1. Current Weather Data - **File Name**: `current_weather.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   location_id|location_name|temperature|condition|humidity|wind_speed|last_updated   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|New York|72|Sunny|65|10|2025-01-20 14:30   2|London|55|Rainy|80|15|2025-01-20 14:30   3|Tokyo|45|Cloudy|72|8|2025-01-20 14:30   ```  ### 2. Forecasts Data - **File Name**: `forecasts.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   forecast_id|location_id|date|high_temp|low_temp|condition|precipitation|humidity   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|1|2025-01-21|75|60|Sunny|0|60   2|1|2025-01-22|68|55|Cloudy|10|70   3|2|2025-01-21|58|48|Rainy|80|85   ```  ### 3. Locations Data - **File Name**: `locations.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   location_id|location_name|latitude|longitude|country|timezone   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|New York|40.7128|-74.0060|USA|EST   2|London|51.5074|-0.1278|UK|GMT   3|Tokyo|35.6762|139.6503|Japan|JST   ```  ### 4. Weather Alerts Data - **File Name**: `alerts.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   alert_id|location_id|alert_type|severity|description|start_time|end_time|is_acknowledged   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|1|Thunderstorm|High|Severe thunderstorm warning in effect until 8 PM|2025-01-20 14:00|2025-01-20 20:00|0   2|2|Fog|Medium|Dense fog advisory in effect until noon tomorrow|2025-01-20 06:00|2025-01-21 12:00|0   3|1|Wind|Medium|High wind advisory with gusts up to 45 mph|2025-01-20 15:00|2025-01-21 09:00|1   ```  ### 5. Air Quality Data - **File Name**: `air_quality.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   aqi_id|location_id|aqi_index|pm25|pm10|no2|o3|last_updated   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|1|45|12.5|35|28|55|2025-01-20 14:30   2|2|67|22.3|48|42|78|2025-01-20 14:30   3|3|120|68.5|95|65|110|2025-01-20 14:30   ```  ### 6. Saved Locations Data - **File Name**: `saved_locations.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   saved_id|user_id|location_id|location_name|is_default   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|1|1|New York|1   2|1|2|London|0   3|2|3|Tokyo|1   ```
  [design_spec.md] # Weather Forecast Web Application Design Specification  This document specifies the structure, routes, data formats, templates, and frontend elements for a comprehensive Weather Forecast web application. It is designed for use with Flask backend and standard HTML templates.  ---  ## General Requirements  - The entire application adheres to snake_case naming conventions for route functions and variables. - Use of `url_for` in templates and redirects ensures route management. - All templates must reside in the `templates/` directory. - Frontend elements have explicit IDs for testing and dynamic JS interactions. - Backend reads data from the provided text files; no database usage is assumed. - Data files: `saved_locations.txt`, `locations.txt`, `current_weather.txt`, `forecasts.txt`, `air_quality.txt`, `alerts.txt`. - Use explicit types and formats for data variables as described below.  ---  ## Routes and Pages  ### 1. Dashboard Page - **Route:** `/dashboard` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** Show overview, saved locations, weather forecast, air quality, and alerts summary. - **Data Files Used:** `saved_locations.txt`, `current_weather.txt`, `air_quality.txt`, `alerts.txt`, `forecasts.txt` - **Template:** `dashboard.html`  #### Template Elements - `h1` with title "Dashboard"  (ID: `dashboard-title`) - Div `saved-locations-list` (ID) - lists saved locations with buttons:   - Buttons with IDs: `select-location-button-{location_id}`, `remove-location-button-{location_id}` - Dropdown `default-location-select` (ID) for default location - Div `current-weather-summary` with elements:   - `temperature-display` (ID) showing temperature with unit   - `weather-condition` (ID) showing condition text   - `humidity-info` (ID) showing humidity %   - `wind-speed-info` (ID) showing wind speed - Table `forecast-table`:   - Columns: Date, High Temp, Low Temp, Condition, Precipitation, Humidity - Div `aqi-display` (ID) showing air quality index number - Div `aqi-description` (ID) showing quality description - Div `alerts-list` (ID) listing active alerts with buttons:   - Buttons with IDs: `acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}` - Checkbox `alert-notifications-toggle` (ID) enable/disable alerts - Button `view-forecast-button` (ID) to navigate forward to forecast page  ### 2. Settings Page - **Route:** `/settings` - **Methods:** GET, POST - **Purpose:** Configure user preferences: temperature unit (Celsius/Fahrenheit), default location. - **Template:** `settings.html`  #### Template Elements - Dropdown `temperature-unit-select` (ID) - Dropdown `default-location-select` (ID) (populated from saved locations) - Button `save-settings-button` (ID) to save preferences  ### 3. Current Weather Page - **Route:** `/weather/current/<int:location_id>` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** Show current weather details for a given location. - **Template:** `current_weather.html`  #### Template Elements - `h1` with location name (ID: `location-name`) - Divs `temperature-display`, `weather-condition`, `humidity-info`, `wind-speed-info` with IDs as above - Button `back-to-dashboard` (ID) to navigate back  ### 4. Weekly Forecast Page - **Route:** `/weather/forecast/<int:location_id>` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** Show 7-day forecast for the location. - **Template:** `weekly_forecast.html`  #### Template Elements - `h1` with location name (ID: `location-name`) - Table `forecast-list` with columns Date, High Temp, Low Temp, Condition, Precipitation, Humidity - Button `back-to-dashboard` (ID) to navigate back  ### 5. Air Quality Page - **Route:** `/air-quality/<int:location_id>` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** Show air quality details for the location. - **Template:** `air_quality.html`  #### Template Elements - Display AQI index, PM2.5, PM10, NO2, O3 levels in a Div with ID: `air-quality-details` - Div `aqi-description` showing description of air quality - Button `back-to-dashboard` (ID)  ### 6. Alerts Page - **Route:** `/alerts/<int:location_id>` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** Show active alerts for the location. - **Template:** `alerts.html`  #### Template Elements - List alerts in Div with ID: `alerts-list` - Each alert row includes Alert Type, Severity, Description, Start Time, End Time - Buttons to acknowledge alert with ID: `acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}` - Button `back-to-dashboard` (ID)  ### 7. Locations Page - **Route:** `/locations` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** Show all saved locations and enable adding/removing locations. - **Template:** `locations.html`  #### Template Elements - Table `locations-table` with columns: Location Name, Latitude, Longitude, Country, Timezone - Buttons for each location:   - `select-location-button-{location_id}`   - `remove-location-button-{location_id}` - Button `add-new-location-button` - Button `back-to-dashboard`  ### 8. Search Page - **Route:** `/search` - **Methods:** GET, POST - **Purpose:** Search locations by name. - **Template:** `search.html`  #### Template Elements - Input Text `location-search-input` (ID) - Button `search-location-button` (ID) - Div `search-results` (ID) listing matching locations  ---  ## Data File Formats  ### saved_locations.txt - Format: `saved_id|user_id|location_id|location_name|is_default` - Example: `1|1|2|London|0`  ### locations.txt - Format: `location_id|location_name|latitude|longitude|country|timezone` - Example: `1|New York|40.7128|-74.0060|USA|EST`  ### current_weather.txt - Format: `location_id|location_name|temperature|condition|humidity|wind_speed|last_updated` - temperature in Celsius or Fahrenheit based on settings - Example: `1|London|12.5|Cloudy|55|15|2025-01-20 14:30`  ### forecasts.txt - Format: `forecast_id|location_id|date|high_temp|low_temp|condition|precipitation|humidity` - Date in `YYYY-MM-DD` - Temperatures follow unit preference - Example: `3|2|2025-01-21|58|48|Rainy|80|85`  ### air_quality.txt - Format: `aqi_id|location_id|aqi_index|pm25|pm10|no2|o3|last_updated` - AQI index integer 0-500 - Example: `3|2|120|68.5|95|65|110|2025-01-20 14:30`  ### alerts.txt - Format: `alert_id|location_id|alert_type|severity|description|start_time|end_time|is_acknowledged` - Severity: Low, Medium, High, Critical - Example: `1|1|Thunderstorm|High|Severe thunderstorm warning|2025-01-20 14:00|2025-01-20 20:00|0`  ---  ## Backend Function Specifications  - All functions use snake_case. - Functions to parse each data file return structured lists of dictionaries with appropriate types. - Route functions load data, parse, and render templates with context dicts. - POST handlers (e.g. settings save) process form data and redirect.  Example: ```python @app.route('/dashboard') def dashboard():     saved_locations = parse_saved_locations()     current_weather = parse_current_weather()     alerts = parse_alerts(active_only=True)     # render with all data     return render_template('dashboard.html', ...) ```  ---  ## Template Use and Context Variables  - Use Jinja2 templating with loops and conditionals. - IDs specified must be present as given. - e.g. for buttons: `<button id="select-location-button-{{ location.location_id }}">Select</button>` - Use `url_for()` in templates for all navigation links.  ---  # End of Specification
================================================================================

# Requirements Document for 'WeatherForecast' Web Application

## 1. Objective
Develop a comprehensive web application named 'WeatherForecast' using Python, with data managed through local text files. The application enables users to view current weather conditions, check weekly forecasts, search for locations, receive weather alerts, and monitor air quality. No authentication required - all features are directly accessible. Note that the website should start from the Dashboard page.

## 2. Language
The required development language for the 'WeatherForecast' application is Python.

## 3. Page Design

The 'WeatherForecast' web application will consist of the following eight pages:

### 1. Dashboard Page
- **Page Title**: Weather Dashboard
- **Overview**: The main hub displaying current weather, quick location access, and navigation to all functionalities.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: dashboard-page** - Type: Div - Container for the dashboard page.
  - **ID: current-weather-summary** - Type: Div - Display of current weather conditions for default location.
  - **ID: search-location-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to location search page.
  - **ID: view-forecast-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to weekly forecast page.
  - **ID: view-alerts-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to weather alerts page.

### 2. Current Weather Page
- **Page Title**: Current Weather
- **Overview**: A page displaying detailed current weather conditions for selected location.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: current-weather-page** - Type: Div - Container for the current weather page.
  - **ID: location-name** - Type: H1 - Display location name.
  - **ID: temperature-display** - Type: Div - Display current temperature.
  - **ID: weather-condition** - Type: Div - Display weather condition (sunny, rainy, cloudy, etc.).
  - **ID: humidity-info** - Type: Div - Display humidity percentage.
  - **ID: wind-speed-info** - Type: Div - Display wind speed.

### 3. Weekly Forecast Page
- **Page Title**: Weekly Forecast
- **Overview**: A page displaying weather forecast for the next 7 days.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: forecast-page** - Type: Div - Container for the forecast page.
  - **ID: forecast-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying daily forecasts with date, high temp, low temp, and condition.
  - **ID: location-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter forecast by location.
  - **ID: forecast-list** - Type: Div - Grid displaying forecast cards for each day.
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

### 4. Location Search Page
- **Page Title**: Search Locations
- **Overview**: A page for users to search and select different locations.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: search-page** - Type: Div - Container for the search page.
  - **ID: location-search-input** - Type: Input - Field to search locations by city name or coordinates.
  - **ID: search-results** - Type: Div - List of search results displaying matching locations.
  - **ID: select-location-button-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to select a location (each result has this).
  - **ID: saved-locations-list** - Type: Div - Display of previously saved locations.

### 5. Weather Alerts Page
- **Page Title**: Weather Alerts
- **Overview**: A page displaying active weather alerts and warnings for selected locations.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: alerts-page** - Type: Div - Container for the alerts page.
  - **ID: alerts-list** - Type: Div - List of all active weather alerts with severity, description, and location.
  - **ID: severity-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter alerts by severity (All, Critical, High, Medium, Low).
  - **ID: location-filter-alerts** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter alerts by location.
  - **ID: acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}** - Type: Button - Button to acknowledge an alert (each alert has this).

### 6. Air Quality Page
- **Page Title**: Air Quality Index
- **Overview**: A page displaying air quality information and pollution levels for locations.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: air-quality-page** - Type: Div - Container for the air quality page.
  - **ID: aqi-display** - Type: Div - Display air quality index value (0-500).
  - **ID: aqi-description** - Type: Div - Display air quality description (Good, Moderate, Unhealthy, etc.).
  - **ID: pollution-details** - Type: Table - Table showing PM2.5, PM10, NO2, and other pollutants.
  - **ID: location-aqi-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter by location.
  - **ID: health-recommendation** - Type: Div - Display health recommendations based on air quality.

### 7. Saved Locations Page
- **Page Title**: Saved Locations
- **Overview**: A page displaying all saved locations with quick weather access.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: saved-locations-page** - Type: Div - Container for the saved locations page.
  - **ID: locations-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying saved locations with current temp and weather condition.
  - **ID: view-location-weather-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view weather for a location (each location has this).
  - **ID: remove-location-button-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to remove saved location (each location has this).
  - **ID: add-new-location-button** - Type: Button - Button to add new location.

### 8. Settings Page
- **Page Title**: Settings
- **Overview**: A page for users to configure temperature units, notification preferences, and default location.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: settings-page** - Type: Div - Container for the settings page.
  - **ID: temperature-unit-select** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select temperature unit (Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin).
  - **ID: default-location-select** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to set default location.
  - **ID: alert-notifications-toggle** - Type: Checkbox - Toggle to enable/disable alert notifications.
  - **ID: save-settings-button** - Type: Button - Button to save settings changes.
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

## 4. Data Storage

The 'WeatherForecast' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:

### 1. Current Weather Data
- **File Name**: `current_weather.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  location_id|location_name|temperature|condition|humidity|wind_speed|last_updated
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|New York|72|Sunny|65|10|2025-01-20 14:30
  2|London|55|Rainy|80|15|2025-01-20 14:30
  3|Tokyo|45|Cloudy|72|8|2025-01-20 14:30
  ```

### 2. Forecasts Data
- **File Name**: `forecasts.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  forecast_id|location_id|date|high_temp|low_temp|condition|precipitation|humidity
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|2025-01-21|75|60|Sunny|0|60
  2|1|2025-01-22|68|55|Cloudy|10|70
  3|2|2025-01-21|58|48|Rainy|80|85
  ```

### 3. Locations Data
- **File Name**: `locations.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  location_id|location_name|latitude|longitude|country|timezone
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|New York|40.7128|-74.0060|USA|EST
  2|London|51.5074|-0.1278|UK|GMT
  3|Tokyo|35.6762|139.6503|Japan|JST
  ```

### 4. Weather Alerts Data
- **File Name**: `alerts.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  alert_id|location_id|alert_type|severity|description|start_time|end_time|is_acknowledged
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|Thunderstorm|High|Severe thunderstorm warning in effect until 8 PM|2025-01-20 14:00|2025-01-20 20:00|0
  2|2|Fog|Medium|Dense fog advisory in effect until noon tomorrow|2025-01-20 06:00|2025-01-21 12:00|0
  3|1|Wind|Medium|High wind advisory with gusts up to 45 mph|2025-01-20 15:00|2025-01-21 09:00|1
  ```

### 5. Air Quality Data
- **File Name**: `air_quality.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  aqi_id|location_id|aqi_index|pm25|pm10|no2|o3|last_updated
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|45|12.5|35|28|55|2025-01-20 14:30
  2|2|67|22.3|48|42|78|2025-01-20 14:30
  3|3|120|68.5|95|65|110|2025-01-20 14:30
  ```

### 6. Saved Locations Data
- **File Name**: `saved_locations.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  saved_id|user_id|location_id|location_name|is_default
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|1|New York|1
  2|1|2|London|0
  3|2|3|Tokyo|1
  ```


=== design_spec.md ===
# Weather Forecast Web Application Design Specification

This document specifies the structure, routes, data formats, templates, and frontend elements for a comprehensive Weather Forecast web application. It is designed for use with Flask backend and standard HTML templates.

---

## General Requirements

- The entire application adheres to snake_case naming conventions for route functions and variables.
- Use of `url_for` in templates and redirects ensures route management.
- All templates must reside in the `templates/` directory.
- Frontend elements have explicit IDs for testing and dynamic JS interactions.
- Backend reads data from the provided text files; no database usage is assumed.
- Data files: `saved_locations.txt`, `locations.txt`, `current_weather.txt`, `forecasts.txt`, `air_quality.txt`, `alerts.txt`.
- Use explicit types and formats for data variables as described below.

---

## Routes and Pages

### 1. Dashboard Page
- **Route:** `/dashboard`
- **Methods:** GET
- **Purpose:** Show overview, saved locations, weather forecast, air quality, and alerts summary.
- **Data Files Used:** `saved_locations.txt`, `current_weather.txt`, `air_quality.txt`, `alerts.txt`, `forecasts.txt`
- **Template:** `dashboard.html`

#### Template Elements
- `h1` with title "Dashboard"  (ID: `dashboard-title`)
- Div `saved-locations-list` (ID) - lists saved locations with buttons:
  - Buttons with IDs: `select-location-button-{location_id}`, `remove-location-button-{location_id}`
- Dropdown `default-location-select` (ID) for default location
- Div `current-weather-summary` with elements:
  - `temperature-display` (ID) showing temperature with unit
  - `weather-condition` (ID) showing condition text
  - `humidity-info` (ID) showing humidity %
  - `wind-speed-info` (ID) showing wind speed
- Table `forecast-table`:
  - Columns: Date, High Temp, Low Temp, Condition, Precipitation, Humidity
- Div `aqi-display` (ID) showing air quality index number
- Div `aqi-description` (ID) showing quality description
- Div `alerts-list` (ID) listing active alerts with buttons:
  - Buttons with IDs: `acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}`
- Checkbox `alert-notifications-toggle` (ID) enable/disable alerts
- Button `view-forecast-button` (ID) to navigate forward to forecast page

### 2. Settings Page
- **Route:** `/settings`
- **Methods:** GET, POST
- **Purpose:** Configure user preferences: temperature unit (Celsius/Fahrenheit), default location.
- **Template:** `settings.html`

#### Template Elements
- Dropdown `temperature-unit-select` (ID)
- Dropdown `default-location-select` (ID) (populated from saved locations)
- Button `save-settings-button` (ID) to save preferences

### 3. Current Weather Page
- **Route:** `/weather/current/<int:location_id>`
- **Methods:** GET
- **Purpose:** Show current weather details for a given location.
- **Template:** `current_weather.html`

#### Template Elements
- `h1` with location name (ID: `location-name`)
- Divs `temperature-display`, `weather-condition`, `humidity-info`, `wind-speed-info` with IDs as above
- Button `back-to-dashboard` (ID) to navigate back

### 4. Weekly Forecast Page
- **Route:** `/weather/forecast/<int:location_id>`
- **Methods:** GET
- **Purpose:** Show 7-day forecast for the location.
- **Template:** `weekly_forecast.html`

#### Template Elements
- `h1` with location name (ID: `location-name`)
- Table `forecast-list` with columns Date, High Temp, Low Temp, Condition, Precipitation, Humidity
- Button `back-to-dashboard` (ID) to navigate back

### 5. Air Quality Page
- **Route:** `/air-quality/<int:location_id>`
- **Methods:** GET
- **Purpose:** Show air quality details for the location.
- **Template:** `air_quality.html`

#### Template Elements
- Display AQI index, PM2.5, PM10, NO2, O3 levels in a Div with ID: `air-quality-details`
- Div `aqi-description` showing description of air quality
- Button `back-to-dashboard` (ID)

### 6. Alerts Page
- **Route:** `/alerts/<int:location_id>`
- **Methods:** GET
- **Purpose:** Show active alerts for the location.
- **Template:** `alerts.html`

#### Template Elements
- List alerts in Div with ID: `alerts-list`
- Each alert row includes Alert Type, Severity, Description, Start Time, End Time
- Buttons to acknowledge alert with ID: `acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}`
- Button `back-to-dashboard` (ID)

### 7. Locations Page
- **Route:** `/locations`
- **Methods:** GET
- **Purpose:** Show all saved locations and enable adding/removing locations.
- **Template:** `locations.html`

#### Template Elements
- Table `locations-table` with columns: Location Name, Latitude, Longitude, Country, Timezone
- Buttons for each location:
  - `select-location-button-{location_id}`
  - `remove-location-button-{location_id}`
- Button `add-new-location-button`
- Button `back-to-dashboard`

### 8. Search Page
- **Route:** `/search`
- **Methods:** GET, POST
- **Purpose:** Search locations by name.
- **Template:** `search.html`

#### Template Elements
- Input Text `location-search-input` (ID)
- Button `search-location-button` (ID)
- Div `search-results` (ID) listing matching locations

---

## Data File Formats

### saved_locations.txt
- Format: `saved_id|user_id|location_id|location_name|is_default`
- Example: `1|1|2|London|0`

### locations.txt
- Format: `location_id|location_name|latitude|longitude|country|timezone`
- Example: `1|New York|40.7128|-74.0060|USA|EST`

### current_weather.txt
- Format: `location_id|location_name|temperature|condition|humidity|wind_speed|last_updated`
- temperature in Celsius or Fahrenheit based on settings
- Example: `1|London|12.5|Cloudy|55|15|2025-01-20 14:30`

### forecasts.txt
- Format: `forecast_id|location_id|date|high_temp|low_temp|condition|precipitation|humidity`
- Date in `YYYY-MM-DD`
- Temperatures follow unit preference
- Example: `3|2|2025-01-21|58|48|Rainy|80|85`

### air_quality.txt
- Format: `aqi_id|location_id|aqi_index|pm25|pm10|no2|o3|last_updated`
- AQI index integer 0-500
- Example: `3|2|120|68.5|95|65|110|2025-01-20 14:30`

### alerts.txt
- Format: `alert_id|location_id|alert_type|severity|description|start_time|end_time|is_acknowledged`
- Severity: Low, Medium, High, Critical
- Example: `1|1|Thunderstorm|High|Severe thunderstorm warning|2025-01-20 14:00|2025-01-20 20:00|0`

---

## Backend Function Specifications

- All functions use snake_case.
- Functions to parse each data file return structured lists of dictionaries with appropriate types.
- Route functions load data, parse, and render templates with context dicts.
- POST handlers (e.g. settings save) process form data and redirect.

Example:
```python
@app.route('/dashboard')
def dashboard():
    saved_locations = parse_saved_locations()
    current_weather = parse_current_weather()
    alerts = parse_alerts(active_only=True)
    # render with all data
    return render_template('dashboard.html', ...)
```

---

## Template Use and Context Variables

- Use Jinja2 templating with loops and conditionals.
- IDs specified must be present as given.
- e.g. for buttons: `<button id="select-location-button-{{ location.location_id }}">Select</button>`
- Use `url_for()` in templates for all navigation links.

---

# End of Specification

Location Search Page - **Page Title**: Search Locations - **Overview**: A page for users to search and select different locations. - **Elements**:   - **ID: search-page** - Type: Div - Container for the search page.   - **ID: location-search-input** - Type: Input - Field to search locations by city name or coordinates.   - **ID: search-results** - Type: Div - List of search results displaying matching locations.   - **ID: select-location-button-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to select a location (each result has this).   - **ID: saved-locations-list** - Type: Div - Display of previously saved locations.  ### 5. Weather Alerts Page - **Page Title**: Weather Alerts - **Overview**: A page displaying active weather alerts and warnings for selected locations. - **Elements**:   - **ID: alerts-page** - Type: Div - Container for the alerts page.   - **ID: alerts-list** - Type: Div - List of all active weather alerts with severity, description, and location.   - **ID: severity-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter alerts by severity (All, Critical, High, Medium, Low).   - **ID: location-filter-alerts** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter alerts by location.   - **ID: acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}** - Type: Button - Button to acknowledge an alert (each alert has this).  ### 6. Air Quality Page - **Page Title**: Air Quality Index - **Overview**: A page displaying air quality information and pollution levels for locations. - **Elements**:   - **ID: air-quality-page** - Type: Div - Container for the air quality page.   - **ID: aqi-display** - Type: Div - Display air quality index value (0-500).   - **ID: aqi-description** - Type: Div - Display air quality description (Good, Moderate, Unhealthy, etc.).   - **ID: pollution-details** - Type: Table - Table showing PM2.5, PM10, NO2, and other pollutants.   - **ID: location-aqi-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter by location.   - **ID: health-recommendation** - Type: Div - Display health recommendations based on air quality.  ### 7. Saved Locations Page - **Page Title**: Saved Locations - **Overview**: A page displaying all saved locations with quick weather access. - **Elements**:   - **ID: saved-locations-page** - Type: Div - Container for the saved locations page.   - **ID: locations-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying saved locations with current temp and weather condition.   - **ID: view-location-weather-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view weather for a location (each location has this).   - **ID: remove-location-button-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to remove saved location (each location has this).   - **ID: add-new-location-button** - Type: Button - Button to add new location.  ### 8. Settings Page - **Page Title**: Settings - **Overview**: A page for users to configure temperature units, notification preferences, and default location. - **Elements**:   - **ID: settings-page** - Type: Div - Container for the settings page.   - **ID: temperature-unit-select** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select temperature unit (Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin).   - **ID: default-location-select** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to set default location.   - **ID: alert-notifications-toggle** - Type: Checkbox - Toggle to enable/disable alert notifications.   - **ID: save-settings-button** - Type: Button - Button to save settings changes.   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ## 4. Data Storage  The 'WeatherForecast' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:  ### 1. Current Weather Data - **File Name**: `current_weather.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   location_id|location_name|temperature|condition|humidity|wind_speed|last_updated   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|New York|72|Sunny|65|10|2025-01-20 14:30   2|London|55|Rainy|80|15|2025-01-20 14:30   3|Tokyo|45|Cloudy|72|8|2025-01-20 14:30   ```  ### 2. Forecasts Data - **File Name**: `forecasts.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   forecast_id|location_id|date|high_temp|low_temp|condition|precipitation|humidity   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|1|2025-01-21|75|60|Sunny|0|60   2|1|2025-01-22|68|55|Cloudy|10|70   3|2|2025-01-21|58|48|Rainy|80|85   ```  ### 3. Locations Data - **File Name**: `locations.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   location_id|location_name|latitude|longitude|country|timezone   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|New York|40.7128|-74.0060|USA|EST   2|London|51.5074|-0.1278|UK|GMT   3|Tokyo|35.6762|139.6503|Japan|JST   ```  ### 4. Weather Alerts Data - **File Name**: `alerts.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   alert_id|location_id|alert_type|severity|description|start_time|end_time|is_acknowledged   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|1|Thunderstorm|High|Severe thunderstorm warning in effect until 8 PM|2025-01-20 14:00|2025-01-20 20:00|0   2|2|Fog|Medium|Dense fog advisory in effect until noon tomorrow|2025-01-20 06:00|2025-01-21 12:00|0   3|1|Wind|Medium|High wind advisory with gusts up to 45 mph|2025-01-20 15:00|2025-01-21 09:00|1   ```  ### 5. Air Quality Data - **File Name**: `air_quality.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   aqi_id|location_id|aqi_index|pm25|pm10|no2|o3|last_updated   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|1|45|12.5|35|28|55|2025-01-20 14:30   2|2|67|22.3|48|42|78|2025-01-20 14:30   3|3|120|68.5|95|65|110|2025-01-20 14:30   ```  ### 6. Saved Locations Data - **File Name**: `saved_locations.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   saved_id|user_id|location_id|location_name|is_default   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   1|1|1|New York|1   2|1|2|London|0   3|2|3|Tokyo|1   ```
  [design_spec.md] # Weather Forecast Web Application Design Specification  This document specifies the structure, routes, data formats, templates, and frontend elements for a comprehensive Weather Forecast web application. It is designed for use with Flask backend and standard HTML templates.  ---  ## General Requirements  - The entire application adheres to snake_case naming conventions for route functions and variables. - Use of `url_for` in templates and redirects ensures route management. - All templates must reside in the `templates/` directory. - Frontend elements have explicit IDs for testing and dynamic JS interactions. - Backend reads data from the provided text files; no database usage is assumed. - Data files: `saved_locations.txt`, `locations.txt`, `current_weather.txt`, `forecasts.txt`, `air_quality.txt`, `alerts.txt`. - Use explicit types and formats for data variables as described below.  ---  ## Routes and Pages  ### 1. Dashboard Page - **Route:** `/dashboard` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** Show overview, saved locations, weather forecast, air quality, and alerts summary. - **Data Files Used:** `saved_locations.txt`, `current_weather.txt`, `air_quality.txt`, `alerts.txt`, `forecasts.txt` - **Template:** `dashboard.html`  #### Template Elements - `h1` with title "Dashboard"  (ID: `dashboard-title`) - Div `saved-locations-list` (ID) - lists saved locations with buttons:   - Buttons with IDs: `select-location-button-{location_id}`, `remove-location-button-{location_id}` - Dropdown `default-location-select` (ID) for default location - Div `current-weather-summary` with elements:   - `temperature-display` (ID) showing temperature with unit   - `weather-condition` (ID) showing condition text   - `humidity-info` (ID) showing humidity %   - `wind-speed-info` (ID) showing wind speed - Table `forecast-table`:   - Columns: Date, High Temp, Low Temp, Condition, Precipitation, Humidity - Div `aqi-display` (ID) showing air quality index number - Div `aqi-description` (ID) showing quality description - Div `alerts-list` (ID) listing active alerts with buttons:   - Buttons with IDs: `acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}` - Checkbox `alert-notifications-toggle` (ID) enable/disable alerts - Button `view-forecast-button` (ID) to navigate forward to forecast page  ### 2. Settings Page - **Route:** `/settings` - **Methods:** GET, POST - **Purpose:** Configure user preferences: temperature unit (Celsius/Fahrenheit), default location. - **Template:** `settings.html`  #### Template Elements - Dropdown `temperature-unit-select` (ID) - Dropdown `default-location-select` (ID) (populated from saved locations) - Button `save-settings-button` (ID) to save preferences  ### 3. Current Weather Page - **Route:** `/weather/current/<int:location_id>` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** Show current weather details for a given location. - **Template:** `current_weather.html`  #### Template Elements - `h1` with location name (ID: `location-name`) - Divs `temperature-display`, `weather-condition`, `humidity-info`, `wind-speed-info` with IDs as above - Button `back-to-dashboard` (ID) to navigate back  ### 4. Weekly Forecast Page - **Route:** `/weather/forecast/<int:location_id>` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** Show 7-day forecast for the location. - **Template:** `weekly_forecast.html`  #### Template Elements - `h1` with location name (ID: `location-name`) - Table `forecast-list` with columns Date, High Temp, Low Temp, Condition, Precipitation, Humidity - Button `back-to-dashboard` (ID) to navigate back  ### 5. Air Quality Page - **Route:** `/air-quality/<int:location_id>` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** Show air quality details for the location. - **Template:** `air_quality.html`  #### Template Elements - Display AQI index, PM2.5, PM10, NO2, O3 levels in a Div with ID: `air-quality-details` - Div `aqi-description` showing description of air quality - Button `back-to-dashboard` (ID)  ### 6. Alerts Page - **Route:** `/alerts/<int:location_id>` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** Show active alerts for the location. - **Template:** `alerts.html`  #### Template Elements - List alerts in Div with ID: `alerts-list` - Each alert row includes Alert Type, Severity, Description, Start Time, End Time - Buttons to acknowledge alert with ID: `acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}` - Button `back-to-dashboard` (ID)  ### 7. Locations Page - **Route:** `/locations` - **Methods:** GET - **Purpose:** Show all saved locations and enable adding/removing locations. - **Template:** `locations.html`  #### Template Elements - Table `locations-table` with columns: Location Name, Latitude, Longitude, Country, Timezone - Buttons for each location:   - `select-location-button-{location_id}`   - `remove-location-button-{location_id}` - Button `add-new-location-button` - Button `back-to-dashboard`  ### 8. Search Page - **Route:** `/search` - **Methods:** GET, POST - **Purpose:** Search locations by name. - **Template:** `search.html`  #### Template Elements - Input Text `location-search-input` (ID) - Button `search-location-button` (ID) - Div `search-results` (ID) listing matching locations  ---  ## Data File Formats  ### saved_locations.txt - Format: `saved_id|user_id|location_id|location_name|is_default` - Example: `1|1|2|London|0`  ### locations.txt - Format: `location_id|location_name|latitude|longitude|country|timezone` - Example: `1|New York|40.7128|-74.0060|USA|EST`  ### current_weather.txt - Format: `location_id|location_name|temperature|condition|humidity|wind_speed|last_updated` - temperature in Celsius or Fahrenheit based on settings - Example: `1|London|12.5|Cloudy|55|15|2025-01-20 14:30`  ### forecasts.txt - Format: `forecast_id|location_id|date|high_temp|low_temp|condition|precipitation|humidity` - Date in `YYYY-MM-DD` - Temperatures follow unit preference - Example: `3|2|2025-01-21|58|48|Rainy|80|85`  ### air_quality.txt - Format: `aqi_id|location_id|aqi_index|pm25|pm10|no2|o3|last_updated` - AQI index integer 0-500 - Example: `3|2|120|68.5|95|65|110|2025-01-20 14:30`  ### alerts.txt - Format: `alert_id|location_id|alert_type|severity|description|start_time|end_time|is_acknowledged` - Severity: Low, Medium, High, Critical - Example: `1|1|Thunderstorm|High|Severe thunderstorm warning|2025-01-20 14:00|2025-01-20 20:00|0`  ---  ## Backend Function Specifications  - All functions use snake_case. - Functions to parse each data file return structured lists of dictionaries with appropriate types. - Route functions load data, parse, and render templates with context dicts. - POST handlers (e.g. settings save) process form data and redirect.  Example: ```python @app.route('/dashboard') def dashboard():     saved_locations = parse_saved_locations()     current_weather = parse_current_weather()     alerts = parse_alerts(active_only=True)     # render with all data     return render_template('dashboard.html', ...) ```  ---  ## Template Use and Context Variables  - Use Jinja2 templating with loops and conditionals. - IDs specified must be present as given. - e.g. for buttons: `<button id="select-location-button-{{ location.location_id }}">Select</button>` - Use `url_for()` in templates for all navigation links.  ---  # End of Specification
================================================================================

# Requirements Document for 'WeatherForecast' Web Application

## 1. Objective
Develop a comprehensive web application named 'WeatherForecast' using Python, with data managed through local text files. The application enables users to view current weather conditions, check weekly forecasts, search for locations, receive weather alerts, and monitor air quality. No authentication required - all features are directly accessible. Note that the website should start from the Dashboard page.

## 2. Language
The required development language for the 'WeatherForecast' application is Python.

## 3. Page Design

The 'WeatherForecast' web application will consist of the following eight pages:

### 1. Dashboard Page
- **Page Title**: Weather Dashboard
- **Overview**: The main hub displaying current weather, quick location access, and navigation to all functionalities.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: dashboard-page** - Type: Div - Container for the dashboard page.
  - **ID: current-weather-summary** - Type: Div - Display of current weather conditions for default location.
  - **ID: search-location-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to location search page.
  - **ID: view-forecast-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to weekly forecast page.
  - **ID: view-alerts-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to weather alerts page.

### 2. Current Weather Page
- **Page Title**: Current Weather
- **Overview**: A page displaying detailed current weather conditions for selected location.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: current-weather-page** - Type: Div - Container for the current weather page.
  - **ID: location-name** - Type: H1 - Display location name.
  - **ID: temperature-display** - Type: Div - Display current temperature.
  - **ID: weather-condition** - Type: Div - Display weather condition (sunny, rainy, cloudy, etc.).
  - **ID: humidity-info** - Type: Div - Display humidity percentage.
  - **ID: wind-speed-info** - Type: Div - Display wind speed.

### 3. Weekly Forecast Page
- **Page Title**: Weekly Forecast
- **Overview**: A page displaying weather forecast for the next 7 days.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: forecast-page** - Type: Div - Container for the forecast page.
  - **ID: forecast-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying daily forecasts with date, high temp, low temp, and condition.
  - **ID: location-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter forecast by location.
  - **ID: forecast-list** - Type: Div - Grid displaying forecast cards for each day.
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

### 4. Location Search Page
- **Page Title**: Search Locations
- **Overview**: A page for users to search and select different locations.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: search-page** - Type: Div - Container for the search page.
  - **ID: location-search-input** - Type: Input - Field to search locations by city name or coordinates.
  - **ID: search-results** - Type: Div - List of search results displaying matching locations.
  - **ID: select-location-button-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to select a location (each result has this).
  - **ID: saved-locations-list** - Type: Div - Display of previously saved locations.

### 5. Weather Alerts Page
- **Page Title**: Weather Alerts
- **Overview**: A page displaying active weather alerts and warnings for selected locations.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: alerts-page** - Type: Div - Container for the alerts page.
  - **ID: alerts-list** - Type: Div - List of all active weather alerts with severity, description, and location.
  - **ID: severity-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter alerts by severity (All, Critical, High, Medium, Low).
  - **ID: location-filter-alerts** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter alerts by location.
  - **ID: acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}** - Type: Button - Button to acknowledge an alert (each alert has this).

### 6. Air Quality Page
- **Page Title**: Air Quality Index
- **Overview**: A page displaying air quality information and pollution levels for locations.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: air-quality-page** - Type: Div - Container for the air quality page.
  - **ID: aqi-display** - Type: Div - Display air quality index value (0-500).
  - **ID: aqi-description** - Type: Div - Display air quality description (Good, Moderate, Unhealthy, etc.).
  - **ID: pollution-details** - Type: Table - Table showing PM2.5, PM10, NO2, and other pollutants.
  - **ID: location-aqi-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter by location.
  - **ID: health-recommendation** - Type: Div - Display health recommendations based on air quality.

### 7. Saved Locations Page
- **Page Title**: Saved Locations
- **Overview**: A page displaying all saved locations with quick weather access.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: saved-locations-page** - Type: Div - Container for the saved locations page.
  - **ID: locations-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying saved locations with current temp and weather condition.
  - **ID: view-location-weather-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view weather for a location (each location has this).
  - **ID: remove-location-button-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to remove saved location (each location has this).
  - **ID: add-new-location-button** - Type: Button - Button to add new location.

### 8. Settings Page
- **Page Title**: Settings
- **Overview**: A page for users to configure temperature units, notification preferences, and default location.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: settings-page** - Type: Div - Container for the settings page.
  - **ID: temperature-unit-select** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select temperature unit (Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin).
  - **ID: default-location-select** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to set default location.
  - **ID: alert-notifications-toggle** - Type: Checkbox - Toggle to enable/disable alert notifications.
  - **ID: save-settings-button** - Type: Button - Button to save settings changes.
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

## 4. Data Storage

The 'WeatherForecast' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:

### 1. Current Weather Data
- **File Name**: `current_weather.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  location_id|location_name|temperature|condition|humidity|wind_speed|last_updated
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|New York|72|Sunny|65|10|2025-01-20 14:30
  2|London|55|Rainy|80|15|2025-01-20 14:30
  3|Tokyo|45|Cloudy|72|8|2025-01-20 14:30
  ```

### 2. Forecasts Data
- **File Name**: `forecasts.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  forecast_id|location_id|date|high_temp|low_temp|condition|precipitation|humidity
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|2025-01-21|75|60|Sunny|0|60
  2|1|2025-01-22|68|55|Cloudy|10|70
  3|2|2025-01-21|58|48|Rainy|80|85
  ```

### 3. Locations Data
- **File Name**: `locations.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  location_id|location_name|latitude|longitude|country|timezone
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|New York|40.7128|-74.0060|USA|EST
  2|London|51.5074|-0.1278|UK|GMT
  3|Tokyo|35.6762|139.6503|Japan|JST
  ```

### 4. Weather Alerts Data
- **File Name**: `alerts.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  alert_id|location_id|alert_type|severity|description|start_time|end_time|is_acknowledged
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|Thunderstorm|High|Severe thunderstorm warning in effect until 8 PM|2025-01-20 14:00|2025-01-20 20:00|0
  2|2|Fog|Medium|Dense fog advisory in effect until noon tomorrow|2025-01-20 06:00|2025-01-21 12:00|0
  3|1|Wind|Medium|High wind advisory with gusts up to 45 mph|2025-01-20 15:00|2025-01-21 09:00|1
  ```

### 5. Air Quality Data
- **File Name**: `air_quality.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  aqi_id|location_id|aqi_index|pm25|pm10|no2|o3|last_updated
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|45|12.5|35|28|55|2025-01-20 14:30
  2|2|67|22.3|48|42|78|2025-01-20 14:30
  3|3|120|68.5|95|65|110|2025-01-20 14:30
  ```

### 6. Saved Locations Data
- **File Name**: `saved_locations.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  saved_id|user_id|location_id|location_name|is_default
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|1|New York|1
  2|1|2|London|0
  3|2|3|Tokyo|1
  ```


=== design_spec.md ===
# Weather Forecast Web Application Design Specification

This document specifies the structure, routes, data formats, templates, and frontend elements for a comprehensive Weather Forecast web application. It is designed for use with Flask backend and standard HTML templates.

---

## General Requirements

- The entire application adheres to snake_case naming conventions for route functions and variables.
- Use of `url_for` in templates and redirects ensures route management.
- All templates must reside in the `templates/` directory.
- Frontend elements have explicit IDs for testing and dynamic JS interactions.
- Backend reads data from the provided text files; no database usage is assumed.
- Data files: `saved_locations.txt`, `locations.txt`, `current_weather.txt`, `forecasts.txt`, `air_quality.txt`, `alerts.txt`.
- Use explicit types and formats for data variables as described below.

---

## Routes and Pages

### 1. Dashboard Page
- **Route:** `/dashboard`
- **Methods:** GET
- **Purpose:** Show overview, saved locations, weather forecast, air quality, and alerts summary.
- **Data Files Used:** `saved_locations.txt`, `current_weather.txt`, `air_quality.txt`, `alerts.txt`, `forecasts.txt`
- **Template:** `dashboard.html`

#### Template Elements
- `h1` with title "Dashboard"  (ID: `dashboard-title`)
- Div `saved-locations-list` (ID) - lists saved locations with buttons:
  - Buttons with IDs: `select-location-button-{location_id}`, `remove-location-button-{location_id}`
- Dropdown `default-location-select` (ID) for default location
- Div `current-weather-summary` with elements:
  - `temperature-display` (ID) showing temperature with unit
  - `weather-condition` (ID) showing condition text
  - `humidity-info` (ID) showing humidity %
  - `wind-speed-info` (ID) showing wind speed
- Table `forecast-table`:
  - Columns: Date, High Temp, Low Temp, Condition, Precipitation, Humidity
- Div `aqi-display` (ID) showing air quality index number
- Div `aqi-description` (ID) showing quality description
- Div `alerts-list` (ID) listing active alerts with buttons:
  - Buttons with IDs: `acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}`
- Checkbox `alert-notifications-toggle` (ID) enable/disable alerts
- Button `view-forecast-button` (ID) to navigate forward to forecast page

### 2. Settings Page
- **Route:** `/settings`
- **Methods:** GET, POST
- **Purpose:** Configure user preferences: temperature unit (Celsius/Fahrenheit), default location.
- **Template:** `settings.html`

#### Template Elements
- Dropdown `temperature-unit-select` (ID)
- Dropdown `default-location-select` (ID) (populated from saved locations)
- Button `save-settings-button` (ID) to save preferences

### 3. Current Weather Page
- **Route:** `/weather/current/<int:location_id>`
- **Methods:** GET
- **Purpose:** Show current weather details for a given location.
- **Template:** `current_weather.html`

#### Template Elements
- `h1` with location name (ID: `location-name`)
- Divs `temperature-display`, `weather-condition`, `humidity-info`, `wind-speed-info` with IDs as above
- Button `back-to-dashboard` (ID) to navigate back

### 4. Weekly Forecast Page
- **Route:** `/weather/forecast/<int:location_id>`
- **Methods:** GET
- **Purpose:** Show 7-day forecast for the location.
- **Template:** `weekly_forecast.html`

#### Template Elements
- `h1` with location name (ID: `location-name`)
- Table `forecast-list` with columns Date, High Temp, Low Temp, Condition, Precipitation, Humidity
- Button `back-to-dashboard` (ID) to navigate back

### 5. Air Quality Page
- **Route:** `/air-quality/<int:location_id>`
- **Methods:** GET
- **Purpose:** Show air quality details for the location.
- **Template:** `air_quality.html`

#### Template Elements
- Display AQI index, PM2.5, PM10, NO2, O3 levels in a Div with ID: `air-quality-details`
- Div `aqi-description` showing description of air quality
- Button `back-to-dashboard` (ID)

### 6. Alerts Page
- **Route:** `/alerts/<int:location_id>`
- **Methods:** GET
- **Purpose:** Show active alerts for the location.
- **Template:** `alerts.html`

#### Template Elements
- List alerts in Div with ID: `alerts-list`
- Each alert row includes Alert Type, Severity, Description, Start Time, End Time
- Buttons to acknowledge alert with ID: `acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}`
- Button `back-to-dashboard` (ID)

### 7. Locations Page
- **Route:** `/locations`
- **Methods:** GET
- **Purpose:** Show all saved locations and enable adding/removing locations.
- **Template:** `locations.html`

#### Template Elements
- Table `locations-table` with columns: Location Name, Latitude, Longitude, Country, Timezone
- Buttons for each location:
  - `select-location-button-{location_id}`
  - `remove-location-button-{location_id}`
- Button `add-new-location-button`
- Button `back-to-dashboard`

### 8. Search Page
- **Route:** `/search`
- **Methods:** GET, POST
- **Purpose:** Search locations by name.
- **Template:** `search.html`

#### Template Elements
- Input Text `location-search-input` (ID)
- Button `search-location-button` (ID)
- Div `search-results` (ID) listing matching locations

---

## Data File Formats

### saved_locations.txt
- Format: `saved_id|user_id|location_id|location_name|is_default`
- Example: `1|1|2|London|0`

### locations.txt
- Format: `location_id|location_name|latitude|longitude|country|timezone`
- Example: `1|New York|40.7128|-74.0060|USA|EST`

### current_weather.txt
- Format: `location_id|location_name|temperature|condition|humidity|wind_speed|last_updated`
- temperature in Celsius or Fahrenheit based on settings
- Example: `1|London|12.5|Cloudy|55|15|2025-01-20 14:30`

### forecasts.txt
- Format: `forecast_id|location_id|date|high_temp|low_temp|condition|precipitation|humidity`
- Date in `YYYY-MM-DD`
- Temperatures follow unit preference
- Example: `3|2|2025-01-21|58|48|Rainy|80|85`

### air_quality.txt
- Format: `aqi_id|location_id|aqi_index|pm25|pm10|no2|o3|last_updated`
- AQI index integer 0-500
- Example: `3|2|120|68.5|95|65|110|2025-01-20 14:30`

### alerts.txt
- Format: `alert_id|location_id|alert_type|severity|description|start_time|end_time|is_acknowledged`
- Severity: Low, Medium, High, Critical
- Example: `1|1|Thunderstorm|High|Severe thunderstorm warning|2025-01-20 14:00|2025-01-20 20:00|0`

---

## Backend Function Specifications

- All functions use snake_case.
- Functions to parse each data file return structured lists of dictionaries with appropriate types.
- Route functions load data, parse, and render templates with context dicts.
- POST handlers (e.g. settings save) process form data and redirect.

Example:
```python
@app.route('/dashboard')
def dashboard():
    saved_locations = parse_saved_locations()
    current_weather = parse_current_weather()
    alerts = parse_alerts(active_only=True)
    # render with all data
    return render_template('dashboard.html', ...)
```

---

## Template Use and Context Variables

- Use Jinja2 templating with loops and conditionals.
- IDs specified must be present as given.
- e.g. for buttons: `<button id="select-location-button-{{ location.location_id }}">Select</button>`
- Use `url_for()` in templates for all navigation links.

---

# End of Specification

# Requirements Document for 'WeatherForecast' Web Application

## 1. Objective
Develop a comprehensive web application named 'WeatherForecast' using Python, with data managed through local text files. The application enables users to view current weather conditions, check weekly forecasts, search for locations, receive weather alerts, and monitor air quality. No authentication required - all features are directly accessible. Note that the website should start from the Dashboard page.

## 2. Language
The required development language for the 'WeatherForecast' application is Python.

## 3. Page Design

The 'WeatherForecast' web application will consist of the following eight pages:

### 1. Dashboard Page
- **Page Title**: Weather Dashboard
- **Overview**: The main hub displaying current weather, quick location access, and navigation to all functionalities.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: dashboard-page** - Type: Div - Container for the dashboard page.
  - **ID: current-weather-summary** - Type: Div - Display of current weather conditions for default location.
  - **ID: search-location-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to location search page.
  - **ID: view-forecast-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to weekly forecast page.
  - **ID: view-alerts-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to weather alerts page.

### 2. Current Weather Page
- **Page Title**: Current Weather
- **Overview**: A page displaying detailed current weather conditions for selected location.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: current-weather-page** - Type: Div - Container for the current weather page.
  - **ID: location-name** - Type: H1 - Display location name.
  - **ID: temperature-display** - Type: Div - Display current temperature.
  - **ID: weather-condition** - Type: Div - Display weather condition (sunny, rainy, cloudy, etc.).
  - **ID: humidity-info** - Type: Div - Display humidity percentage.
  - **ID: wind-speed-info** - Type: Div - Display wind speed.

### 3. Weekly Forecast Page
- **Page Title**: Weekly Forecast
- **Overview**: A page displaying weather forecast for the next 7 days.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: forecast-page** - Type: Div - Container for the forecast page.
  - **ID: forecast-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying daily forecasts with date, high temp, low temp, and condition.
  - **ID: location-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter forecast by location.
  - **ID: forecast-list** - Type: Div - Grid displaying forecast cards for each day.
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

### 4. Location Search Page
- **Page Title**: Search Locations
- **Overview**: A page for users to search and select different locations.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: search-page** - Type: Div - Container for the search page.
  - **ID: location-search-input** - Type: Input - Field to search locations by city name or coordinates.
  - **ID: search-results** - Type: Div - List of search results displaying matching locations.
  - **ID: select-location-button-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to select a location (each result has this).
  - **ID: saved-locations-list** - Type: Div - Display of previously saved locations.

### 5. Weather Alerts Page
- **Page Title**: Weather Alerts
- **Overview**: A page displaying active weather alerts and warnings for selected locations.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: alerts-page** - Type: Div - Container for the alerts page.
  - **ID: alerts-list** - Type: Div - List of all active weather alerts with severity, description, and location.
  - **ID: severity-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter alerts by severity (All, Critical, High, Medium, Low).
  - **ID: location-filter-alerts** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter alerts by location.
  - **ID: acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}** - Type: Button - Button to acknowledge an alert (each alert has this).

### 6. Air Quality Page
- **Page Title**: Air Quality Index
- **Overview**: A page displaying air quality information and pollution levels for locations.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: air-quality-page** - Type: Div - Container for the air quality page.
  - **ID: aqi-display** - Type: Div - Display air quality index value (0-500).
  - **ID: aqi-description** - Type: Div - Display air quality description (Good, Moderate, Unhealthy, etc.).
  - **ID: pollution-details** - Type: Table - Table showing PM2.5, PM10, NO2, and other pollutants.
  - **ID: location-aqi-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter by location.
  - **ID: health-recommendation** - Type: Div - Display health recommendations based on air quality.

### 7. Saved Locations Page
- **Page Title**: Saved Locations
- **Overview**: A page displaying all saved locations with quick weather access.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: saved-locations-page** - Type: Div - Container for the saved locations page.
  - **ID: locations-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying saved locations with current temp and weather condition.
  - **ID: view-location-weather-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view weather for a location (each location has this).
  - **ID: remove-location-button-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to remove saved location (each location has this).
  - **ID: add-new-location-button** - Type: Button - Button to add new location.

### 8. Settings Page
- **Page Title**: Settings
- **Overview**: A page for users to configure temperature units, notification preferences, and default location.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: settings-page** - Type: Div - Container for the settings page.
  - **ID: temperature-unit-select** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select temperature unit (Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin).
  - **ID: default-location-select** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to set default location.
  - **ID: alert-notifications-toggle** - Type: Checkbox - Toggle to enable/disable alert notifications.
  - **ID: save-settings-button** - Type: Button - Button to save settings changes.
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

## 4. Data Storage

The 'WeatherForecast' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:

### 1. Current Weather Data
- **File Name**: `current_weather.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  location_id|location_name|temperature|condition|humidity|wind_speed|last_updated
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|New York|72|Sunny|65|10|2025-01-20 14:30
  2|London|55|Rainy|80|15|2025-01-20 14:30
  3|Tokyo|45|Cloudy|72|8|2025-01-20 14:30
  ```

### 2. Forecasts Data
- **File Name**: `forecasts.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  forecast_id|location_id|date|high_temp|low_temp|condition|precipitation|humidity
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|2025-01-21|75|60|Sunny|0|60
  2|1|2025-01-22|68|55|Cloudy|10|70
  3|2|2025-01-21|58|48|Rainy|80|85
  ```

### 3. Locations Data
- **File Name**: `locations.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  location_id|location_name|latitude|longitude|country|timezone
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|New York|40.7128|-74.0060|USA|EST
  2|London|51.5074|-0.1278|UK|GMT
  3|Tokyo|35.6762|139.6503|Japan|JST
  ```

### 4. Weather Alerts Data
- **File Name**: `alerts.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  alert_id|location_id|alert_type|severity|description|start_time|end_time|is_acknowledged
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|Thunderstorm|High|Severe thunderstorm warning in effect until 8 PM|2025-01-20 14:00|2025-01-20 20:00|0
  2|2|Fog|Medium|Dense fog advisory in effect until noon tomorrow|2025-01-20 06:00|2025-01-21 12:00|0
  3|1|Wind|Medium|High wind advisory with gusts up to 45 mph|2025-01-20 15:00|2025-01-21 09:00|1
  ```

### 5. Air Quality Data
- **File Name**: `air_quality.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  aqi_id|location_id|aqi_index|pm25|pm10|no2|o3|last_updated
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|45|12.5|35|28|55|2025-01-20 14:30
  2|2|67|22.3|48|42|78|2025-01-20 14:30
  3|3|120|68.5|95|65|110|2025-01-20 14:30
  ```

### 6. Saved Locations Data
- **File Name**: `saved_locations.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  saved_id|user_id|location_id|location_name|is_default
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|1|New York|1
  2|1|2|London|0
  3|2|3|Tokyo|1
  ```

Alerts Page\n- **Route:** `/alerts/<int:location_id>`\n- **Methods:** GET\n- **Purpose:** Show active alerts for the location.\n- **Template:** `alerts.html`\n\n#### Template Elements\n- List alerts in Div with ID: `alerts-list`\n- Each alert row includes Alert Type, Severity, Description, Start Time, End Time\n- Buttons to acknowledge alert with ID: `acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}`\n- Button `back-to-dashboard` (ID)\n\n### 7. Locations Page\n- **Route:** `/locations`\n- **Methods:** GET\n- **Purpose:** Show all saved locations and enable adding/removing locations.\n- **Template:** `locations.html`\n\n#### Template Elements\n- Table `locations-table` with columns: Location Name, Latitude, Longitude, Country, Timezone\n- Buttons for each location:\n  - `select-location-button-{location_id}`\n  - `remove-location-button-{location_id}`\n- Button `add-new-location-button`\n- Button `back-to-dashboard`\n\n### 8. dicts.\n- POST handlers (e.g. settings save) process form data and redirect.\n\nExample:\n```python\n@app.route('/dashboard')\ndef dashboard():\n    saved_locations = parse_saved_locations()\n    current_weather = parse_current_weather()\n    alerts = parse_alerts(active_only=True)\n    # render with all data\n    return render_template('dashboard.html', ...)\n```\n\n---\n\n## Template Use and Context Variables\n\n- Use Jinja2 templating with loops and conditionals.\n- IDs specified must be present as given.\n- e.g. for buttons: `<button id=\"select-location-button-{{ location.location_id }}\">Select</button>`\n- Use `url_for()` in templates for all navigation links.\n\n---\n\n# End of Specification"})

=== user_task_description ===

# Requirements Document for 'WeatherForecast' Web Application

## 1. Objective
Develop a comprehensive web application named 'WeatherForecast' using Python, with data managed through local text files. The application enables users to view current weather conditions, check weekly forecasts, search for locations, receive weather alerts, and monitor air quality. No authentication required - all features are directly accessible. Note that the website should start from the Dashboard page.

## 2. Language
The required development language for the 'WeatherForecast' application is Python.

## 3. Page Design

The 'WeatherForecast' web application will consist of the following eight pages:

### 1. Dashboard Page
- **Page Title**: Weather Dashboard
- **Overview**: The main hub displaying current weather, quick location access, and navigation to all functionalities.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: dashboard-page** - Type: Div - Container for the dashboard page.
  - **ID: current-weather-summary** - Type: Div - Display of current weather conditions for default location.
  - **ID: search-location-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to location search page.
  - **ID: view-forecast-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to weekly forecast page.
  - **ID: view-alerts-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to weather alerts page.

### 2. Current Weather Page
- **Page Title**: Current Weather
- **Overview**: A page displaying detailed current weather conditions for selected location.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: current-weather-page** - Type: Div - Container for the current weather page.
  - **ID: location-name** - Type: H1 - Display location name.
  - **ID: temperature-display** - Type: Div - Display current temperature.
  - **ID: weather-condition** - Type: Div - Display weather condition (sunny, rainy, cloudy, etc.).
  - **ID: humidity-info** - Type: Div - Display humidity percentage.
  - **ID: wind-speed-info** - Type: Div - Display wind speed.

### 3. Weekly Forecast Page
- **Page Title**: Weekly Forecast
- **Overview**: A page displaying weather forecast for the next 7 days.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: forecast-page** - Type: Div - Container for the forecast page.
  - **ID: forecast-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying daily forecasts with date, high temp, low temp, and condition.
  - **ID: location-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter forecast by location.
  - **ID: forecast-list** - Type: Div - Grid displaying forecast cards for each day.
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

### 4. Location Search Page
- **Page Title**: Search Locations
- **Overview**: A page for users to search and select different locations.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: search-page** - Type: Div - Container for the search page.
  - **ID: location-search-input** - Type: Input - Field to search locations by city name or coordinates.
  - **ID: search-results** - Type: Div - List of search results displaying matching locations.
  - **ID: select-location-button-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to select a location (each result has this).
  - **ID: saved-locations-list** - Type: Div - Display of previously saved locations.

### 5. Weather Alerts Page
- **Page Title**: Weather Alerts
- **Overview**: A page displaying active weather alerts and warnings for selected locations.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: alerts-page** - Type: Div - Container for the alerts page.
  - **ID: alerts-list** - Type: Div - List of all active weather alerts with severity, description, and location.
  - **ID: severity-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter alerts by severity (All, Critical, High, Medium, Low).
  - **ID: location-filter-alerts** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter alerts by location.
  - **ID: acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}** - Type: Button - Button to acknowledge an alert (each alert has this).

### 6. Air Quality Page
- **Page Title**: Air Quality Index
- **Overview**: A page displaying air quality information and pollution levels for locations.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: air-quality-page** - Type: Div - Container for the air quality page.
  - **ID: aqi-display** - Type: Div - Display air quality index value (0-500).
  - **ID: aqi-description** - Type: Div - Display air quality description (Good, Moderate, Unhealthy, etc.).
  - **ID: pollution-details** - Type: Table - Table showing PM2.5, PM10, NO2, and other pollutants.
  - **ID: location-aqi-filter** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to filter by location.
  - **ID: health-recommendation** - Type: Div - Display health recommendations based on air quality.

### 7. Saved Locations Page
- **Page Title**: Saved Locations
- **Overview**: A page displaying all saved locations with quick weather access.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: saved-locations-page** - Type: Div - Container for the saved locations page.
  - **ID: locations-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying saved locations with current temp and weather condition.
  - **ID: view-location-weather-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to view weather for a location (each location has this).
  - **ID: remove-location-button-{location_id}** - Type: Button - Button to remove saved location (each location has this).
  - **ID: add-new-location-button** - Type: Button - Button to add new location.

### 8. Settings Page
- **Page Title**: Settings
- **Overview**: A page for users to configure temperature units, notification preferences, and default location.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: settings-page** - Type: Div - Container for the settings page.
  - **ID: temperature-unit-select** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select temperature unit (Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin).
  - **ID: default-location-select** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to set default location.
  - **ID: alert-notifications-toggle** - Type: Checkbox - Toggle to enable/disable alert notifications.
  - **ID: save-settings-button** - Type: Button - Button to save settings changes.
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

## 4. Data Storage

The 'WeatherForecast' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:

### 1. Current Weather Data
- **File Name**: `current_weather.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  location_id|location_name|temperature|condition|humidity|wind_speed|last_updated
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|New York|72|Sunny|65|10|2025-01-20 14:30
  2|London|55|Rainy|80|15|2025-01-20 14:30
  3|Tokyo|45|Cloudy|72|8|2025-01-20 14:30
  ```

### 2. Forecasts Data
- **File Name**: `forecasts.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  forecast_id|location_id|date|high_temp|low_temp|condition|precipitation|humidity
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|2025-01-21|75|60|Sunny|0|60
  2|1|2025-01-22|68|55|Cloudy|10|70
  3|2|2025-01-21|58|48|Rainy|80|85
  ```

### 3. Locations Data
- **File Name**: `locations.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  location_id|location_name|latitude|longitude|country|timezone
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|New York|40.7128|-74.0060|USA|EST
  2|London|51.5074|-0.1278|UK|GMT
  3|Tokyo|35.6762|139.6503|Japan|JST
  ```

### 4. Weather Alerts Data
- **File Name**: `alerts.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  alert_id|location_id|alert_type|severity|description|start_time|end_time|is_acknowledged
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|Thunderstorm|High|Severe thunderstorm warning in effect until 8 PM|2025-01-20 14:00|2025-01-20 20:00|0
  2|2|Fog|Medium|Dense fog advisory in effect until noon tomorrow|2025-01-20 06:00|2025-01-21 12:00|0
  3|1|Wind|Medium|High wind advisory with gusts up to 45 mph|2025-01-20 15:00|2025-01-21 09:00|1
  ```

### 5. Air Quality Data
- **File Name**: `air_quality.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  aqi_id|location_id|aqi_index|pm25|pm10|no2|o3|last_updated
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|45|12.5|35|28|55|2025-01-20 14:30
  2|2|67|22.3|48|42|78|2025-01-20 14:30
  3|3|120|68.5|95|65|110|2025-01-20 14:30
  ```

### 6. Saved Locations Data
- **File Name**: `saved_locations.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  saved_id|user_id|location_id|location_name|is_default
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  1|1|1|New York|1
  2|1|2|London|0
  3|2|3|Tokyo|1
  ```
